name: Prepositions and Contractions
summary: Italian prepositions merge with the article into single contracted words.
explanation:
When a preposition meets a definite article, they fuse into one word. This happens with a (to/at), di (of), da (from), in (in), and su (on).

So a + il becomes al, di + la becomes della, in + il becomes nel, su + le becomes sulle. The pattern is regular once you see it: vado al bar (I go to the bar), la casa di Maria becomes il libro dell'amico.

Two prepositions, con (with) and per (for), normally do not contract: con il treno, per la festa.

examples.native: È la casa della mia amica.

examples.en: The cat is in the garden.
The book is on the table.

commonMistakes: Writing the preposition and article separately. In + il must become nel, not in il.
Contracting con and per. They normally stay separate.
Forgetting the double l in forms like della, dello, sullo.
Using a instead of in for cities versus countries: a Roma but in Italia.
Mixing up di (of) with da (from), which change the meaning completely.
tip: Build a small grid of preposition rows against article columns once; after that the contractions become automatic.
